licence.p: This work is licensed under the Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 Unported Licence. To view a copy of the licence, visit https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/ or send a letter to Creative Commons, 444 Castro Street, Suite 900, Mountain View, California, 94041, USA.

projectDesc.p: The project DHARMA has received funding from the European Research Council (ERC) under the European Union's Horizon 2020 research and innovation programme (grant agreement no 809994).

prefixDef.p: Public URIs with the prefix bib to point to a Zotero Group Library named ERC-DHARMA whose data are open to the public.
Internal URIs using the part prefix to point to person elements in the DHARMA_idListMembers.xml file.

div.p: * Omجگ namo nؤپrؤپyaل¹‡ؤپya.

lg.l: vidyud yatra maل¹‡i-dyutiل¸¥ phaل¹‡i-pater vؤپlendur indrؤپyudhaل¹پ
bhإ«yؤپd vaل¸¥ sa bhavؤپrtti-tؤپpa-bhiduraل¸¥ إ›ambhoل¸¥ kaparddؤپmvu-daل¸¥.

kahlؤپre hata-mohyatؤپ
rati-patؤپv eko â€™ham eveti dhؤ«ل¸¥,

yasyؤپmؤ« Amrج¥tؤپtmanaل¸¥ samudayanty ؤپإ›u prakؤپ
إ›ؤپj jagaty

yaإ›obhiل¸¥ إ›obhante paridhi-pari
ل¹‡addhؤپ Iva diإ›aل¸¥,

yaإ› cetoma
yam eva إ›aurya-vijayؤ« datvauل¹£adhaل¹پ taT-kل¹£aل¹‡ؤپd

akل¹£ؤ«ل¹‡ؤپ racayؤپأ± cakؤپra vaإ›agؤپل¸¥
svasminآ· pareل¹£ؤپل¹پ إ›riyaل¸¥.

-svantؤپ yؤپ
ntu kathan na nؤپma ripavas tasya prayogؤپl layamآ·.

yair ؤپtma-prativimvite â€™pi nipatat-patre â€™pi
caأ±cat-trج¥ل¹‡e â€™py
advaitena yatas tato â€™pi sa paro devaل¸¥ paraل¹پ vؤ«kل¹£yate..

div.p:
sa khalu إ›rؤ«-vikra
mapura-samؤپvؤپsita-إ›rؤ«maj-jaya-skandhؤپvؤپrؤپT mahؤپrؤپjؤپdhirؤپja-إ›rؤ«-vallؤپlasena
-deva-pؤپdؤپnudhyؤپta-parameإ›vara-paramavaiل¹£ل¹‡ava-paramabhaل¹­ل¹­ؤپraka-mahؤپrؤپjؤپdhirؤپja
-إ›rؤ«mal-lakل¹£maل¹‡asena-devaل¸¥ kuإ›alؤ«. samupagatؤپإ›eل¹£a-rؤپja-rؤپjanyaka-rؤپjأ±ؤ«
-rؤپل¹‡aka-rؤپjaputra-rؤپjؤپmؤپtya-purohita-mahؤپdharmmؤپdhyakل¹£a-mahؤپsؤپndhivigrahi
ka-mahؤپsenؤپpati-mahؤپmudrؤپdhikrج¥ta-ؤ€ntaraل¹…ga-vrج¥haduparika-mahؤپkل¹£apaل¹­alika
-mahؤپpratؤ«hؤپra-mahؤپbhogika-mahؤپpؤ«lupati-mahؤپgaل¹‡astha-daussؤپdhika-cauro
ddharaل¹‡ika-nauvalahastyaإ›vagomahiل¹£ؤپjؤپvikؤپdivyؤپprج¥taka-gaulmika-daل¹‡ل¸چapؤپإ›i
ka-daل¹‡ل¸چanؤپyaka-viل¹£ayapatvy-ؤپdؤ«n ananyؤپل¹پإ› ca sakala-rؤپja-pؤپdopajؤ«vino â€™dhyakل¹£a-pra
cؤپroktؤپn ihؤپkؤ«rttitؤپnآ· caل¹­ل¹­a-bhaل¹­ل¹­a-jؤپtؤ«yؤپnآ· janapradؤپnآ· kل¹£etrakarؤپل¹پإ› ca vrؤپ
hmaل¹‡ؤپnآ· vrؤپhmaل¹‡ottarؤپnآ· yathؤپrhaل¹پ mؤپnayati vodhayati samؤپdiإ›ati ca.

matam a
stu bhavatؤپل¹پ. yathؤپ إ›rؤ«-pauل¹‡ل¸چravarddhana-bhukty-antaل¸¥pؤپti-varendryؤپnآ·* pإ«rvve vuddha-vihؤپrؤ«-deva
tؤپ-nikara-deyؤپrmmaل¹‡a-bhإ«my-ؤپل¸چhؤپvؤپpa-pإ«rvvؤپliل¸¥ sؤ«mؤپ. dakل¹£iل¹‡e nicaل¸چa-hؤپra-pu
ل¹£kariل¹‡ؤ« sؤ«mؤپ. paإ›cime nandiharipؤپ-kuل¹‡ل¸چؤ« sؤ«mؤپ. Uttare mollؤپل¹‡a-khؤپل¸چؤ« sؤ«mؤپ. I
tthaل¹پ catuل¸¥-sؤ«mؤپvacchinnas tatratya-deإ›a-vyavahؤپra-nalena deva-go-pathؤپdy-asؤپra-bhإ«-vahiل¸¥
paأ±conmؤپnؤپdhika-viل¹پإ›aty-uttarؤپل¸چhؤپvؤپpa-إ›ataikؤپtmakaل¸¥ saل¹پvvatsareل¹‡a kaparddaka-pu
rؤپل¹‡a-sؤپrddha-إ›ataikotpattiko velahiل¹£ل¹­ؤ«grؤپmؤ«ya-bhإ«-bhؤپgaل¸¥ sa-jhؤپل¹­a-viل¹­apaل¸¥
sa-jala-sthalaل¸¥ sa-garttoل¹£araل¸¥ sa-guvؤپka-nؤپrikelaل¸¥ sahya-daإ›ؤپparؤپdhaل¸¥ pari
rhrج¥ta-sarvva-pؤ«ل¸چo â€™caل¹­ل¹­a-bhaل¹­ل¹­a-praveإ›o â€™kiأ±ciT-pragrؤپhyas trج¥ل¹‡a-pإ«ti-gocara
-paryantaل¸¥ hutؤپإ›anadevaإ›armmaل¹‡aل¸¥ prapautrؤپya mؤپrkkaل¹‡ل¸چeyadevaإ›armmaل¹‡aل¸¥ pautrؤپya la
kل¹£mؤ«dharadevaإ›armmaل¹‡aل¸¥ putrؤپya bharadvؤپja-sagotrؤپya bhؤپradvؤپja-ؤ€ل¹…girasa
-vؤپrhaspatya-pravarؤپya sؤپmaveda-kauthuma-إ›ؤپkhؤپ-caraل¹‡ؤپnuل¹£ل¹­hؤپyine hemؤپإ›va-ra
tha-mahؤپ-dؤپnؤپcؤپrya-إ›rؤ«-ؤھإ›varadevaإ›armmaل¹‡e puل¹‡ye â€™hani vidhivad udaka-pإ«
rvvakaل¹پ bhagavantaل¹پ إ›rؤ«man-nؤپrؤپyaل¹‡a-bhaل¹­ل¹­ؤپrakam uddiإ›ya mؤپtؤپ-pitror ؤپtmanaإ› ca pu
ل¹‡ya-yaإ›o â€™bhivrج¥ddhaye datta-hemؤپإ›yva-dratha-mahؤپ-dؤپne dakل¹£iل¹‡ؤپtvenotsrج¥jya ؤ€
-candrؤپrkka-kل¹£iti-sama-kؤپlaل¹پ yؤپvaT bhإ«mi-cchidra-nyؤپyena tؤپmra-إ›ؤپsanؤ«-krج¥tya pra
datto â€™smؤپbhiل¸¥. tad bhavadbhiل¸¥ sarvvair evؤپnumatntavyamآ·. bhؤپvibhir api nrج¥pati
bhir apaharaل¹‡e naraka-pؤپta-bhyؤپT pؤپlane dharmma-gauravؤپt pؤپlanؤ«yamآ·.

bha
vanti cؤپtra dharmmؤپnuإ›aل¹پsinaل¸¥ إ›lokؤپل¸¥,

lg.l: yasya yasya yadؤپ bhإ«mis tasya tasya tadؤپ phalaل¹پ.

bhإ«miل¹پ yaل¸¥ pratigrج¥hnؤپti yaإ› ca bhإ«miل¹پ praya
cchati,

sva-dattؤپل¹پ para-dattؤپm vؤپ yo hare
ta vasundharؤپmآ·,
sa viل¹£ل¹­hؤپyؤپل¹پ krج¥mir bhإ«tvؤپ pitrج¥bhis saha pacyate.

div.p: saل¹پ 2 bhؤپdra-dine 28
إ›rؤ« ni mahؤپsؤپل¹پ ni .

app.note: Banerji takes ل¸¥ as a sign of interpunction.
Banerji takes ل¸¥ as a sign of interpunction.
As suggested by Banerji and Majumdar, this seems to be a kؤپkapada symbol indicating the attempt of emendation by the scribe, who seems to have forgotten to insert the correction.
Majumdar suggests that mmaل¹‡a is a substitute for mؤپla
Need to be dإ«takaل¹پ for metrical consideration.

div.p: Success! Oل¹پ, homage to Nؤپrؤپyaل¹‡a!

In which flash is like brilliance of the jewel of the Lord of Snakes إڑeل¹£a, the new moon is like a rainbow, water is the River of Heaven Gaل¹…gؤپ, the garland of white skulls is like a row of swans, breath is suppressed by a habit of meditation, and elevation of hairs is superior. May that cloud-like knotted hair of إڑambhu become the one destroying grief of existence by heat for you!

The delight in the ocean, the closeness to cutting of sorrow among a flock of partridges, the destroyed delusion in a waterlily, the reflection as â€œI am the only oneâ€‌ in the Husband of Pleasure Kؤپma. These rise immediately in this world from lustre of that one made of nectar the Moon. May that light of the Moon, bowing to the tradition of meditation by Atri, stay in the happy person!

With creepers that were lustre of their foot nails shining by water that was beams from crowns of a crore of kings bowed for service, the enjoyers of the earth kings, removing burns caused by poison that was energy of their enemies, were then clearly born in the lineage of the Lord of Plants the Moon.

By snows that were the fame of his arms, spreading as far as the Kaumؤپra Mountain and flowing in various regions, he exhibited withering of lotuses that were faces of enemy kings. Hemantasena, who had ripe and dense praiseworthy characters furnished with a series of merits from land and stream that was the Sena lineage, belonging to the lineage of them kings of the lunar lineage, was just distinctly born.

Even now, quarters shine by fame belonging to him, going with accumulated energy of his arms, as if they are wrapped with a garment. Then that Vijayasena, the victor, the husband of the earth surrounded by waves of the four oceans skillful in play of girdles, was born.

Then Vallؤپlasena, the obstacle for the completion of the Kali Age, an active sole traveller on the path of the Vedas, and like the army with shapes in both station and moving, was born. He, the brave winner, after giving medicine made of his heart at the same moment, by his eye made the Fortune of enemy kings into an obedient wife on himself.

Due to desire for enjoyment of the characters of a group of women in other quarters to be traversed, by the lords of quarters filled with such and such powers, he was produced by bestowal of their parts. Hence King illustrious Lakل¹£amaل¹‡asena, who had liquid of poison that was his enemy suppressed by heat of his arms, who was the abode of the duties of royal class, and whose boundary was benevolence, was born.

Why then should not enemies deceptions, deprived of their territories liberated from objects of senses and having a good end by being killed by made devoted to him only, because of fear of endless imprisonment eternal bondage, go to destruction rest due to contrivance of connection with him? By them, wherever, on their own mirrored image reflection on ؤپtman, also on a descending letter falling leaf, and on moving grass, nothing but that highest king god was looked at, without doubt with non-duality.

From the illustrious camp of victory pitched at illustrious Vikramapura. Parameإ›vara, devout worshiper of Viل¹£ل¹‡u, paramabhaل¹­ل¹­ؤپraka mahؤپrؤپjؤپdhirؤپja illustrious Lakل¹£maل¹‡asenadeva, healthy, accepted by his majesty mahؤپrؤپjؤپdhirؤپja illustrious Vallؤپlasena, honours, announces and orders suitably all the approached rؤپja, rؤپjanyaka, rؤپjأ±ؤ«, rؤپل¹‡aka, rؤپjaputra, rؤپjؤپmؤپtya, purohita, mahؤپdharmؤپdhyakل¹£a, mahؤپsؤپndhivigrahika, mahؤپsenؤپpati, mahؤپmudrؤپdhikrج¥ta, ؤپntaraل¹…ga, brج¥haduparika, mahؤپkل¹£apaل¹­alika, mahؤپpratؤ«hؤپra, mahؤپbhogika, mahؤپpؤ«lupati, mahؤپgaل¹‡astha, daussؤپdhika, cauroddharaل¹‡ika, official in charge of fleet, elephants, horses, cows, buffaloes, goat, sheep and so on, gaulmika, daل¹‡ل¸چapؤپإ›ika, daل¹‡ل¸چanؤپyaka, viل¹£ayapati and so on, and all other dependants on the royal feet, those declared of positions of officials, unnamed here, the ones belonging to caل¹­ل¹­as and bhaل¹­ل¹­as, people and cultivators, brؤپhmaل¹‡as and best brؤپhmaل¹‡as as follows:

â€œIt should be approved by you. In Varendrؤ« belonging to illustrious Pauل¹‡ل¸چravardhana bhukti. To the east, the eastern embankment of 1 ؤپل¸چhؤپvؤپpa of land for 1 armaل¹‡a droل¹‡a of production to be given to a group of deities of the Buddha vihؤپrؤ« is the border; to the south, the pond of Nicؤپل¸چahara is the border; to the west, the reservoir of Nandiharipؤپ is the border; in the north, the canal of Mollؤپل¹‡a is the border. A portion of land belonging to Velahiل¹£ل¹­ؤ«grؤپma, demarcated by the four borders as such, measured by a nala which is custom in this region, excluding unprofitable land like that of deity and cattle track, consisting of 120 ؤپل¸چhؤپvؤپpas added with 5 unmؤپnas, production of 150 purؤپل¹‡as in cowries annually, was given by us with forest and bush, with watering place, with ditch and saline land, with areca nut and coconut trees, with ten offences to be tolerated, exempted from all burdens, without entrance of caل¹­ل¹­as and bhaل¹­ل¹­as, without anything taken away, as far as grass field and pasture, to illustrious ؤھإ›varadevaإ›arman, the ؤپcؤپrya of the great gift of golden horses and chariot, the great grandson of Hutؤپإ›anadevaإ›arman, the grandson of Mؤپrkandeyadevaإ›arman, the son of Lakل¹£mؤ«dharadevaإ›arman, belonging to Bharadvؤپja gotra and Bhؤپradvؤپja, ؤ€ل¹…girasa and Bؤپrhaspatya pravara, performing the observance of Kauthuma إ›ؤپkhؤپ of the Sؤپmaveda, on an auspicious day, after pouring water according to rule, in the name of illustrious Lord Nؤپrؤپyaل¹‡a, for the increase of merit and fame of parents and myself, after discharging it for being a reward of the given great gift of golden horses and chariot, for the same period as the moon, the sun and the earth exist, by the rule of land reclamation, after making a copperplate edict. Therefore, it should be consented to by you all. And it should also be protected by future kings from fear of hell in case of violation and from respect for merit in case of protection.â€‌
And here are verses instructing dharma as follows:
The earth was given by many kings beginning with Sagara. To the one to whom the land belongs, belongs then the merit.
The one who gains land and the one who gives land, both of them with meritorious deeds are surely going to heaven.
Either given by himself or given by the others, the one who stole land, he would be boiled with his ancestors after becoming an insect in excreta.
After thus considering wealth and human life fragile as a drop of water on lotus petal, also after knowing all that is said, the meritorious deeds of others should not be destroyed by human beings.
Illustrious Lakل¹£maل¹‡asena, the king, made Nؤپrؤپyaل¹‡adatta sؤپndhivigrahika a messenger here in the donation of the edict to ؤھإ›vara.
Year 2 month Bhؤپdra day 28
Confirmed by the illustrious king. Confirmed by mahؤپsؤپل¹پdhivigrahika.

First notified with an eye copy, transcription and translation by E. Vesey Westmacott. Then re-edited and published by R. D. Banerji first in Bengali and then by him in English. Then re-edited again by N. G. Majumdar99-105. Now re-edited by Ryosuke Furui, based on the digital photographs provided by Bangiya Sahitya Parishat.
